task_description: Produce consensus planning artifacts for one-shot full-backend DDD migration from deep-interview spec.

review_summary.architect: Approved after tightening dependency-direction rules and adding schema/dependency non-goals.
review_summary.critic: Approved after confirming acceptance criteria, verification path, pre-mortem, and execution handoff guidance are concrete.

residual_risks: User-defined completion remains structure-first, so runtime regressions do not block task completion.
One-shot migration across api and admin-api creates a large review and integration surface.
Architecture conformance relies on grep/review/build checks rather than dedicated enforcement tooling.
recommended_execution_mode: ralph or team depending desired parallelism
key_revision: Switched target package topology from horizontal global layers to domain-first vertical slices with internal DDD layers; added lightweight-DDD floor and stronger architecture checks.
final_decision: Preserve server/api and server/admin-api as runtime modules, but reorganize each domain as a vertical slice with internal interfaces/application/domain/infrastructure layers; keep common cross-cutting only.
summary: Approved ralplan for one-shot backend DDD migration using domain-first vertical slices with internal interfaces/application/domain/infrastructure layering inside api and admin-api.
